reasoning: Both audits correctly identified the PKGBUILD uses trusted sources with pinned checksums, standard AppImage handling procedures, and no dangerous operations during makepkg execution. The skipped AppImage binary poses no risk during build/install.
coverage_issues: Both models skipped reviewing the AppImage binary (nextcloud-*.AppImage) despite it being the primary payload. While not executed during build, it contains runtime code that could pose user risks.
No review of .INSTALL scripts (if present) - though none exist in this package
false_negatives: Potential runtime risks in the AppImage binary were not assessed (e.g., embedded scripts, unexpected network calls). This is outside makepkg scope but relevant for user security.
learnings: Improve binary handling: For AppImage packages, consider extracting contents (desktop files/icons) for review instead of full skip
Enhance coverage rules: Prioritize review of any .install hooks even if not present in this case
Add warning annotations for packages distributing precompiled binaries
Improve model guidance: Clarify that binary runtime risks are out-of-scope for AUR build safety audits
